changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage deep tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing.
verified.notes: Engine Ventures spun out as a separate investment firm from The Engine (nonprofit incubator/accelerator) in 2023. The Engine was founded by MIT in 2016. Sources do not provide current AUM, flagship fund name, or named senior partners for Engine Ventures specifically.

changesProposed.notes: Engine Ventures spun out as a separate investment firm from The Engine (nonprofit incubator/accelerator) in 2023. The Engine was founded by MIT in 2016. Sources do not provide current AUM, flagship fund name, or named senior partners for Engine Ventures specifically.

changesProposed.name: Sequoia Capital

verified.name: Sequoia Capital
verified.thesis: Sequoia specializes in seed stage, early stage, and growth stage investments in private technology companies across clean tech, consumer internet, crypto, financial services, healthcare, mobile, and robotics sectors. The firm operates an evergreen fund structure for its U.S. and European business that allows it to remain involved with companies after their public market debuts.
verified.notes: In November 2025, Roelof Botha was succeeded by Alfred Lin and Pat Grady as joint managing partners. Sequoia separated into three independent entities in 2024: Sequoia (U.S. and Europe), Peak XV Partners (Southeast Asia and India), and HongShan (China).

changes.current: Sequoia specializes in seed stage, early stage, and growth stage investments in private technology companies across clean tech, consumer internet, crypto, financial services, healthcare, mobile, and robotics sectors. The firm operates an evergreen fund structure for its U.S. and European business that allows limited partners to pull liquidity instead of following traditional 10-year fund cycles.
changes.verified: Sequoia specializes in seed stage, early stage, and growth stage investments in private technology companies across clean tech, consumer internet, crypto, financial services, healthcare, mobile, and robotics sectors. The firm operates an evergreen fund structure for its U.S. and European business that allows it to remain involved with companies after their public market debuts.
